data.title: US Justice Department fires two who worked for Biden-era official targeted by Trump, sources say
data.description:
By Sarah N. Lynch

Two federal prosecutors who once worked for former Deputy Attorney General Lisa Monaco have been fired, as President Donald Trump's administration expands its targeting of people with any connection to the federal cases against him during his years out of power, three people familiar with the matter said.

Trump in recent days has also pressured Monaco's new employer, Microsoft , to fire her.

The DOJ on Wednesday fired Michael Ben'Ary, a 20-year veteran who led the national security section in the Eastern District of Virginia, after he was targeted online by a far-right influencer about his prior work with Monaco.

Last week, the department fired Maya Song, a former first assistant who worked in the same office. She had previously served as a counsel to Monaco and had also been targeted online.

The Justice Department declined comment, citing the government shutdown. Song and Ben'Ary could not immediately be reached.

Monaco, who serves as Microsoft's president of global affairs, served in the Justice Department while Special Counsel Jack Smith prosecuted Trump over his retention of classified documents and efforts to overturn the 2020 election. She also coordinated the Justice Department's response to the January 6, 2021, attacks by Trump supporters on the U.S. Capitol.

Microsoft declined to comment.

The Eastern District of Virginia handles high-profile terrorism and espionage cases, and recently brought federal charges against former FBI Director James Comey.

data.title: Karbon Capital Partners Files for IPO
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Karbon Capital Partners, a blank-check company with former senator Joe Manchin chairing its board, has filed for an initial public offering.

The special-purpose acquisition company plans to offer 30 million units for $10 apiece. Each unit consists of one share and a third of a redeemable warrant, with each whole warrant entitling the holder to buy a share for $11.50.

Karbon plans to focus its search for a business combination target in the energy industry, including power generation and energy infrastructure, as that is where its management team has experience, it said in a Thursday filing. Karbon is on the lookout for companies that are benefiting from the increase in energy demand from AI data centers and liquefied natural gas.

Joe Manchin, former senator for West Virginia, is chair of Karbon's board but is not an employee. Karbon's executive officers include Thomas Karam, who is board chair of the natural gas producer EQT, and Jeffrey Zajkowski, the former head of North American equity capital markets at JPMorgan.

Karbon intends to list shares on Nasdaq under the symbol KBON.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: CACI International Secures $73 Million U.S. Air Force Contract
data.description:
By Terell Wright

CACI International has won a $73 million, five-year contract to continue its work modernizing the U.S. Air Force's integrated broadcast service.

The national security technology company said Thursday that the contract from the Defense Department will focus on upgrading the Air Force's network for transmitting time-sensitive tactical and strategic intelligence and targeting data across multiple domains, including air, ground and space, for the Defense Department and U.S. allies.

The contract will support the Integrated Broadcast Services-Network Services, which are used to transmit intelligence for the Air Force electronically.

CACI Chief Executive John Mengucci said the company's software-defined solutions that broaden the Air Force's network capabilities will bolster the nation's national security and allow it to maintain superiority.

Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com

data.title: Planemaker Embraer delivers 62 jets in Q3, up 5% from a year earlier
data.description:
Brazilian planemaker Embraer said on Thursday it delivered 62 aircraft in the third quarter of 2025, about 5% higher than the 59 deliveries in the same period last year.

The world's third-largest planemaker delivered 20 commercial aircraft in the quarter, a 25% increase year-on-year, and 41 executive jets, in line with a year earlier, Embraer said in a securities filing.

The Phenom 300 represented nearly half of all the executive jets delivered by Embraer in the quarter through the end of September, according to the company.

Embraer also delivered one defense aircraft in the period, a KC-390 Millennium, compared with two deliveries in the defense segment a year ago.

The company is set to release its third-quarter financial results on November 4, according to its website.

Embraer forecasts deliveries in 2025 of between 77 and 85 commercial, and 145 to 155 executive jets. In the year through the third quarter, Embraer delivered 46 commercial jets and 102 executive jets.

data.title: XERIANT, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Xeriant, Inc., a company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of disruptive technologies in advanced materials and aerospace, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss: $1.65 million. The decreased net loss was primarily related to less operating expenses in the current year, offset by more interest expense in the prior year.

Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $0.00. This reflects the potential dilution that could occur if securities or other contracts to issue common stock were exercised or converted into common stock.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Xeriant, Inc. is focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of disruptive technologies in advanced materials and aerospace. The company aims to integrate these technologies across multiple industrial sectors, partnering with visionary companies to accelerate its mission.

Advanced Materials: The company is developing eco-friendly advanced materials, particularly in nanotechnology, with applications across various industries. The NEXBOARD™, a patent-pending composite construction panel made from plastic and fiber waste, is a key product aimed at replacing traditional construction materials.

Aerospace Focus: Xeriant is targeting high-growth-potential technologies in aerospace, including advanced air mobility, unmanned aerial systems, and sustainable propulsion systems. The company is exploring partnerships and acquisitions to enhance sustainability, performance, and safety in aerospace technologies.

New Product Development: In 2024, Xeriant began testing production processes for NEXBOARD™ to ensure competitiveness and scalability. The company is also developing proprietary flame-retardant technology for polymers in recycled materials.

Intellectual Property: Xeriant has filed a provisional patent for a multilayered fire-resistant polymer composite and is pursuing trademark applications for NEXBOARD™ and DUREVER™.

Joint Ventures: The company had a joint venture with XTI Aircraft to complete the preliminary design review of an eVTOL fixed-wing aircraft, which was terminated in 2023. Xeriant is involved in legal proceedings related to this venture.

Future Outlook: Xeriant plans to expand its portfolio of advanced materials and products, potentially setting up manufacturing facilities in the U.S. for NEXBOARD™ production. The company is in discussions with investment banks for financing through green bond issuances.

Market Opportunity: The company is positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for green construction materials and advanced aerospace technologies, with significant market opportunities in both sectors.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Xeriant, Inc. is focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of disruptive technologies in advanced materials and aerospace. The company is actively pursuing partnerships and strategic interests in visionary companies to accelerate its mission. A key initiative includes the development of NEXBOARD™, an eco-friendly composite construction panel, and the exploration of manufacturing and branding opportunities for advanced materials. The company is also involved in legal proceedings to enforce agreements related to its joint venture with XTI Aircraft, which could impact its strategic direction.

Capital Management: The company has been primarily funded through private investors and convertible notes. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, Xeriant issued convertible debt totaling $676,000 and converted $971,000 in principal and $97,050 in accrued interest into common stock. The company has a significant amount of convertible notes payable, including a $5.9 million note with Auctus Fund LLC, which is in default. The company is engaged in ongoing litigation related to this note. As of June 30, 2025, Xeriant had a negative working capital of $8,712,005 and a net loss of $1,646,898, raising substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern.

Future Outlook: Xeriant plans to raise additional capital through equity or debt to fund operations and pursue its business plan. The company is exploring the issuance of green bonds to finance manufacturing facilities for NEXBOARD™. Management is in discussions with an investment bank for potential financing. The company aims to develop profitable operations and is dependent on raising funds to continue its strategic initiatives. The outcome of ongoing litigation and the ability to secure additional funding will significantly influence the company's future prospects.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Xeriant, Inc. faces significant challenges in establishing manufacturing operations for its eco-friendly products, such as NEXBOARD™, which require substantial capital investment and certification for use in the construction industry. The company's strategy involves partnerships and acquisitions, but the success of these initiatives is uncertain due to potential technological hurdles and market competition.

Risk Factors: Xeriant is in its development stage with a limited operating history, no sales, and ongoing operating losses. The company anticipates continued losses and requires substantial additional capital, which may not be available on acceptable terms. The company's financial position is further strained by obligations under the Auctus Fund LLC Senior Secured Note, which could result in asset loss or shareholder dilution if not resolved favorably. Legal proceedings related to this note and other disputes, such as with XTI Aircraft Company, add to the financial and operational risks.

The company faces significant industry competition, particularly in the development of advanced materials and aerospace technologies. The ability to manage growth effectively, attract and retain key personnel, and develop competitively priced products are critical challenges. Supply chain disruptions, economic changes, and public health conditions like pandemics could adversely impact operations.

Intellectual property risks are significant, with potential misappropriation of proprietary rights and the need for litigation to protect these rights. The company may also face claims of infringement, which could result in costly legal battles and impact product development and commercialization efforts.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the need for additional financing to fund operations and execute the business plan. The company is exploring strategic alliances and licensing arrangements to mitigate financial risks and accelerate market entry. However, the success of these strategies is contingent on favorable market conditions and the ability to secure necessary capital.

Market Risk Disclosures: Xeriant is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in raw material prices and potential changes in regulatory environments that could impact product certification and market acceptance. The company's financial performance is sensitive to these external factors, which could affect its ability to achieve profitability and sustain operations.

SEC Filing: XERIANT, INC. [ XERI ] - 10-K - Oct. 02, 2025

data.title: OpenAI's Latest Funding Deal Returns Focus to AI Economy, Lifting U.S. Stocks — WSJ
data.description:
By Dalvin Brown

OpenAI's recent funding deal and stunning valuation helped lift U.S. stocks to another record closing high.

OpenAI's latest investment round valued the privately held AI company at about $500 billion, and two tech heavyweights, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, signed on as memory-chip partners for its "Stargate" infrastructure project. Semiconductor stocks rose, and investors looked past the potential fallout from a government shutdown that is expected to stretch into next week.

"The main, dominant story is AI and technology," said Keith Lerner, co-chief investment officer at Truist Advisory Services.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite rose 0.4%, while the broader S&P 500 index added 0.1%. Both benchmarks closed at all-time highs for the 30th time this year. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.2%, or 78.62 points. It was the Dow's 10th record close of 2025.

The concentrated gains showed investors sticking with megacap tech while waiting out the pause in government data on the nation's economy.

"Today was the first real day that we got to experience a data void," said Vail Hartman, U.S. rates strategist at BMO Capital Markets. With jobless claims unavailable Thursday and Friday's employment report likely delayed, traders turned to alternative gauges such as the Chicago Fed's labor indicators.

Prediction markets suggest bettors think the shutdown will last until at least the end of next week, which would delay key economic data such as the monthly jobs report.

"Investors have shifted focus to earnings season in two weeks," Lerner added. Semiconductors look particularly insulated from Washington's disruptions, keeping the sector tied more to AI headlines.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index rose 1.9%, with Nvidia up 0.9% and Broadcom up 1.4%.

Some strategists cautioned that the tech rally may be getting ahead of fundamentals. "That's mostly momentum," said Robert Haworth, senior investment strategist at U.S. Bank Wealth Management. "We'll need earnings to confirm demand."

Next up is Friday's ISM services index, one of the few indicators that doesn't rely on government data. Any signs the shutdown timeline is lengthening could test the market's calm.

With official data sidelined, investors are turning to corporate guidance. Banks kick off reporting in two weeks, and early commentary on loan growth and consumer health could matter more than any delayed government release.

Write to Dalvin Brown at dalvin.brown@wsj.com

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1354 ET - Now that OpenAI has reached a $500 billion valuation, analysts see a few different avenues for the company to keep growing. UBS says OpenAI is likely to introduce more consumer AI agents on its ChatGPT platform, similar to the partnerships with Etsy and Shopify it announced earlier this week. That could include a travel booking agent, the analysts say. There is also a potential for an AI browser launch, posing competition to Google Chrome. The analysts say they wouldn't be surprised to see OpenAI also enhance its coding tools and other new features for creative AI and ChatGPT for business uses. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1153 ET - FICO's new credit score delivery system, which allows resellers to bypass credit bureaus to deliver FICO scores to customers, will reduce clarity in the mortgage industry, say UBS analysts. "We believe this change adds substantial uncertainty to a sector that has already been undergoing heightened volatility amid a series of potential regulatory changes," the analysts say. It is still unclear how the change will impact the sourcing of datasets FICO uses to generate scores, which are "provided by the credit bureaus FICO aims to displace." The change could impact competitive dynamics depending on how VantageScore, a credit score model owned by the credit bureaus, responds. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0700 ET - Prosus is likely to use its resources and established food-delivery infrastructure to support Just Eat Takeaway, ING analyst Marc Hesselink writes in a note. The technology investor has said its offer for the Amsterdam-based food-delivery company as unconditional. However, the analyst notes that a successful turnaround in Just Eat's operational performance would serve as a key validation of Prosus's strategic pivot from being a passive investor to actively operating its portfolio companies. Prosus shares are up 1.6% at 61.47 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0542 ET - SM Entertainment is expected to post 3Q revenue of KRW309 billion, up 28% on year, and operating profit of KRW46 billion, up 243%, CGS International analyst Jiwoo Oh says in a note. Concerts, merchandising and licensing are core earnings drivers, supported by strong demand from a loyal fanbase. Oh notes the Korean entertainment company has the most appeal in China compared to its sector peers, citing strong IPs and collaboration potential with Tencent. CGS forecasts the company to deliver a record-high full-year revenue of KRW1.14 trillion, up 15% on year, and operating profit of KRW171 billion. CGS maintains an add rating and a target price of KRW180,000 on SM's stock, which last closed at KRW127,100.(jason.chau@wsj.com)

0425 ET - Shares of European companies that supply chip-making tools are on the rise after Samsung Electronics struck an initial agreement with OpenAI to establish AI infrastructure under the Stargate project. The partnership raises hopes among investors that Samsung will need to purchase more chip-making tools. Shares of ASML Holding--which sells equipment to Samsung--are up 4.2% in Amsterdam. Shares of ASML's smaller rival, ASM International, are up 5.8%. Shares of BE Semiconductor Industries, the Dutch supplier of semiconductor assembly equipment, rise 6.3%. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0408 ET - Kuaishou Technology is set to benefit from its video-generation business, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. "We see the release of [OpenAI's video generation model] Sora 2 attracting industry attention on its strong video generation capabilities," they say. The Chinese short-video company has a video-generation model called Kling, which is a bright spot for the company. Video generation is a huge market, the analysts add. Jefferies keeps a buy rating on Kuaishou and raises its target price to HK$106.00 from HK$94.00. Shares are 8.6% higher at HK$91.65. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0016 ET - The U.S. proposal for equal chip production has no benefit to Taiwanese companies, Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee says. The proposed supply chain shift would greatly diminish the cost advantage of TSMC and other chipmakers due to higher labor and construction costs in the U.S., he says. If the 50-50 split applies on the chip level instead of the company level, it could lead to a big wave of potentially wasteful mature-node investments in the U.S., he notes. Meanwhile, a bigger presence of Taiwanese chipmakers increases risks of intellectual property leakage, the analyst says. "It's also highly impossible for the 50-50 split to happen within Trump's term, as building fabs and preparing them for production takes 3-4 years in the best circumstances," he adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2325 ET - The feasibility of the U.S. proposal for Taiwan to shift more chip production to the U.S. hinges on multiple factors, says Chiang Min-yen at Taiwan's Research Institute for Democracy, Society and Emerging Technology. Whether the U.S. can ensure sufficient local talent pipeline, U.S. customers' willingness to accept higher costs and pay a premium, and other suppliers' capability to scale up to support the ecosystem are all critical to any supply-chain shift, he says. According to current plans, the U.S. should be able to capture at least 20% of global advanced-chip capacity by 2030, Chiang notes. However, if this shift were to slow down TSMC's pace of innovation and create risks of being surpassed by China, then such a plan should be considered infeasible, he says. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2304 ET - Singapore's small- and mid-cap stocks could rise in 4Q after a recent pullback, says DBS Group Research. Fund deployment and anticipated set of measures to overhaul the local equity market are supporting the gains in these counters, analysts Kee Yan Yeo and Fang Boon Foo write in a note. Potential beneficiaries include iFAST Corp. and UMS Integration, while DBS expects their industrial sector picks, such as SIA Engineering, SATS and Seatrium, to deliver growth rates above the sector average. DBS maintains its year-end target for the Straits Times Index at 4430, pegging key support levels at 4250 and 4175. The STI hit a record intraday high of 4376.78 before paring some gains to 4374.41.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)

2221 ET - Xiaomi's electric-vehicle business should maintain solid momentum, Deutsche Bank analyst Bin Wang says in commentary. Xiaomi reported September EV deliveries of 40,000 units, the highest level since it entered the market, the analyst notes. Wang expects the margin on Xiaomi's new initiatives business segment, which includes the EV business, to further improve in 2H. This should be supported by stronger sales of higher-margin models and operating leverage as sales volumes grow, Wang says. The analyst keeps a buy rating on Xiaomi with a target price of HK$77.00. Shares last traded at HK$55.00. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

1902 ET [Dow Jones]--Australian stocks are set to rise at the open, with ASX SPI200 futures up by 0.5% before the bell. The market will have tailwinds from Wall Street, where stocks extended recent gains. "Financial markets were largely unperturbed as the U.S. government entered a shutdown," Westpac senior economist Mantas Vanagas says in a note. Rather, sentiment has been buoyed by expectations of more Fed rate cuts this year. Australia's gold stocks are likely to get a boost from a continued rally in gold prices, which trade at a record high. Other minerals including copper, silver and iron ore are also higher, providing tailwinds for the resource-rich market, although oil prices are down. Ahead of trading, ARN Media named Chief Operating Officer Michael Stephenson as its next CEO, succeeding Ciaran Davis. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1155 ET - Credit bureau Experian is expected to see limited direct impact from FICO's changes in the near term, Bernstein's Will Kirkness and Filippo Giardini write. U.S.-based analytics software company FICO said it will sell its credit score directly to mortgage lenders directly, instead of offering the score through credit bureaus. That said, lenders can still use the bureaus to access the scores, the analysts say. However, over the longer term, the shift could create margin pressure and weigh on the company's valuation due to regulatory uncertainty and potential changes in market structure. Experian shares are down 4.2% at 3,520 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1153 ET - FICO's new credit score delivery system, which allows resellers to bypass credit bureaus to deliver FICO scores to customers, will reduce clarity in the mortgage industry, say UBS analysts. The change could impact competitive dynamics depending on how VantageScore, a credit score model owned by the credit bureaus, responds. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0739 ET - London-listed credit rating agency Experian is facing an emerging rival, AJ Bell's Russ Mould writes in a note. California-based analytics-software company FICO has launched a new service that it believes could reduce U.S. mortgage brokers' and lenders' reliance on credit agencies like Experian for assessing consumer credit risk, he says. Experian shares are down 5.4% at 3,477 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

2304 ET - Singapore's small- and mid-cap stocks could rise in 4Q after a recent pullback, says DBS Group Research. Fund deployment and anticipated set of measures to overhaul the local equity market are supporting the gains in these counters, analysts Kee Yan Yeo and Fang Boon Foo write in a note. Potential beneficiaries include iFAST Corp. and UMS Integration, while DBS expects their industrial sector picks, such as SIA Engineering, SATS and Seatrium, to deliver growth rates above the sector average. DBS maintains its year-end target for the Straits Times Index at 4430, pegging key support levels at 4250 and 4175. The STI hit a record intraday high of 4376.78 before paring some gains to 4374.41.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)

2155 ET - Bank Syariah Indonesia is likely facing an earnings slowdown, Maybank Sekuritas Indonesia analysts say in a report. The bank has probably incurred higher operating expenses from ongoing investments in digital security, infrastructure and databases while achieving milder fee-based income growth, the analysts note. The bank's strong financing growth also weighed on margins early in the year, as it was compelled to raise deposits in a tighter liquidity environment. The brokerage cuts its 2025 and 2026 net profit forecasts for the bank by 6.8% and 7.9%, respectively. It lowers its target price on the stock to IDR3,350.00 from IDR3,600.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares last closed at IDR2,590.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2149 ET - Westpac is likely to sweeten its share buyback program, but it's not a certainty, says Morgan Stanley. It forecasts a A$1 billion top-up to Westpac's on-market buyback in 2026. That view reflects Westpac's healthy capital buffer, lower interest-rate risk in its banking book, and the prospect of a A$500 million capital release from a reduction in its operational risk overlay. "However, the combination of stronger loan growth, higher UNITE spending, and a new CFO could see Westpac take a more measured approach to capital management," analyst Richard E. Wiles says. UNITE refers to Westpac's technology overhaul. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2052 ET - Bank Central Asia's share-price correction of around 21% year to date may present a buying opportunity, says DBS Group Research in a note. Analysts Muhammad Nurkholis Syafruddin and Rui Wen Lim say the Indonesian bank's net interest income, which could improve into 2026, could be the key share-price driver over loan growth, provisions and noninterest income. Bank Central Asia's earnings growth will likely continue to be supported by healthy loan yields and resilient funding, they say. Meanwhile, its stable net interest margin could offset moderating loan volumes. DBS maintains its buy rating and target of IDR12,000. Shares closed 1.6% lower at IDR7,500. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1214 ET - The timing of Occidental Petroleum's deal to sell its petrochemical business to Berkshire Hathaway doesn't appear ideal, TD Cowen analysts say in a research note earlier this week, following reports that the companies were holding discussions. While the transaction will help the oil-and-gas company pay down debt, it comes as capital expenditures tied to the petrochemical unit's multiyear growth initiatives are peaking. This means the free cash flow inflection expected in coming years will be lost, the analysts say. Investors will also likely question the timing given the OxyChem's recent earnings weakness, they add. Occidental shares fall 6.5% following confirmation of the sale Thursday. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1000 ET - Oil prices keep falling as a government shutdown clouds the U.S. economic outlook while politicians show no signs of moving toward a deal. High U.S. crude inventories, coupled with expectations of increased production by OPEC+, fuel concerns of excessive supply. "The near-term fear of OPEC+ increasing production further is keeping a 'sell the rallies' mentality for most traders," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler writes. The WTI November contract falls 0.5% to $61.50 a barrel. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0927 ET - Occidental Petroleum is selling its petrochemical business to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7 billion, a move CEO Vicki Hollub says will resolve an outstanding issue weighing on the company's market value. "Our shareholders are very happy with our portfolio, and happy with our performance," she says in an interview on CNBC. "The problem has been getting our debt down faster." Occidental's acquisitions of Anadarko Petroleum and CrownRock in 2019 and 2024, respectively, saddled the company with debt. Occidental plans to use $6.5 billion in proceeds from the Berkshire deal to pay down debt, achieving its target level of principal debt below $15 billion. This move will "unlock our stock, and allow shareholders to feel more comfortable--hopefully--to add to their positions and others to come in," Hollub says. Occidental rises 1% premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0341 ET - SSE's earnings guidance were a touch soft versus consensus, Jefferies analyst Ahmed Farman says in a research note. The energy group forecasts EPS of between 33 pence and 37 pence for the six months to Sept. 30, 2025, in line with seasonal averages, Farman says. SSE EPS guidance is tracking at the lower end, which is a minor negative, the analyst says. However, the company's full-year expectations remain unchanged, he says. Over the medium term, SSE is investing heavily in networks, with adjusted capex in 1H expected to increase 60% on year to 1.1 billion pounds, the analyst adds. Shares fall 2.7% to 1,718 pence.(maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

2304 ET - Singapore's small- and mid-cap stocks could rise in 4Q after a recent pullback, says DBS Group Research. Fund deployment and anticipated set of measures to overhaul the local equity market are supporting the gains in these counters, analysts Kee Yan Yeo and Fang Boon Foo write in a note. Potential beneficiaries include iFAST Corp. and UMS Integration, while DBS expects their industrial sector picks, such as SIA Engineering, SATS and Seatrium, to deliver growth rates above the sector average. DBS maintains its year-end target for the Straits Times Index at 4430, pegging key support levels at 4250 and 4175. The STI hit a record intraday high of 4376.78 before paring some gains to 4374.41.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)

2225 ET - Petronas Gas' internal reorganization could ultimately benefit shareholders, MBSB Research says in a note. The plan to separate its regulated and non-regulated businesses may streamline operations, reduce redundancies and optimize processes, it says. It will not affect issued share capital, substantial holdings, or materially impact earnings or net assets. Expected efficiency gains, cost reductions and improved capital management should enhance long-term shareholder value, though MBSB does not expect any short-term impact. MBSB maintains a neutral rating on Petronas Gas and a target price of MYR19.25 on the stock, which is 0.1% lower at MYR18.58. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2123 ET - Thai Oil's asset monetization plan looks positive, Thanachart Securities' Yupapan Polpornprasert says in a research report. Under the plan, it intends to sell crude oil tanks, a single buoy mooring, a lorry loading station, and land at Thailand's Si Racha to a new subsidiary, the analyst notes. The transaction is slated to be completed by 4Q, generating THB37.4 billion in lease rights, with net proceeds to Thai Oil of around THB18.2 billion after adjustments. The net proceeds would be used for debt repayment, mitigating risks of a credit downgrade and higher interest rates for the complex-refining company. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to THB41.00 from THB34.00 to partly reflect the asset monetization, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares last closed at THB35.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1434 ET - AGL Energy looks oversold to Citi. Investors are cautious because AGL is undertaking a capital-intensive transformation that will require A$5 billion of investment through FY 2030, Citi says. "We argue the debate for investors is not whether Origin Energy deserves a premium, but what catalyst can trigger a rotation back into AGL," analyst Tom Wallington says. This can happen when investors gain greater visibility on AGL's battery build-out as it shifts away from coal. "Our thesis is that the market is overly discounting AGL's risks at this point in the cycle and as execution is de-risked, sentiment should shift from skepticism to a recognition of the transformation value," Citi adds. AGL ended Wednesday at A$9.22. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1432 ET - Gold futures turn lower, with the front-month contract pulling back after rising to new highs for five consecutive sessions. Today's finish is still the third-highest close in gold's history. The market appears to be taking a breath after gold prices climbed closer to $4,000 a troy ounce. Traders remain on edge due to the government shutdown and its effect on data releases, says Felipe Barragán of Pepperstone in a note. "Without important upcoming economic data, traders will closely monitor oil prices as well as U.S. government developments," says Barragán. Front-month gold futures fall 0.7% to $3,839.70 a troy ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0624 ET - Crude palm oil ended higher on stronger soybean oil prices and solid export performance, according to David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Ng sees support for prices at 4,400 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,580 ringgit a ton. Market sentiment is also lifted by overnight gains on Wall Street, Kenanga Futures notes. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for December delivery rises 62 ringgit to MYR4,450 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0414 ET - Shares in London-listed miners rose on the back of a boost to copper prices driven by supply-concerns stemming from an accident at Indonesia's Grasberg mine. According to ANZ Research analysts, the mudslide at the project--one of the world's largest--has taken 3% of global mine supply offline. Freeport-McMoRan, the mine's operator, reduced its sales guidance by 35% for 2026, with early estimates pointing at the mine not returning to full capacity until early 2027. Copper on the London Metal Exchange is trading around $10,409 a metric ton. Rio Tinto shares are up 1.4%, Anglo American rises 1.1%, and Antofagasta is up 1%. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

2120 ET - It is likely Dyno Nobel will close the Phosphate Hill fertilizer plant next year, according to Morgans analyst Belinda Moore, who reckons it will be hard for the company to find a buyer of the facility. Dyno Nobel says a sale process for Phosphate Hill is continuing, but has signaled it will close the plant by the end of September 2026 if it can't find a buyer by March 31. "If DNL can sell this business, we don't expect the proceeds to be meaningful given the buyer will need to fund the next turnaround which will cost at least A$100 million," says Moore. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2111 ET - Morgans analyst Belinda Moore laments the sale of Dyno Nobel's fertilizers businesses "for poor prices while earnings are high." The broker upgrades FY 2025-2026 fertilizers earnings estimates because of increased prices, but Moore notes that is "somewhat irrelevant" given Dyno Nobel is selling those operations. "The removal of fertilizers earnings is highly EPS dilutive (especially at these prices), however we recognize that as a pure play explosives business, DNL will become a much simpler story," Moore says. Morgans raises its target on the stock to A$3.16 from A$2.82. It reiterates a hold rating. Moore says Morgans prefers Orica for exposure to explosives. Dyno Nobel is down 0.9% at A$3.22. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1935 ET - The new shape of Dyno Nobel's business is becoming clearer as it sheds its fertilizers assets and focuses on explosives, says Citi analyst William Park. Improved clarity triggers a lift in Citi's target on the stock to A$3.50, from A$2.55. The bank reiterates a neutral rating. "There is no shortage of structural tailwinds that should be supportive of DNL's growth trajectory," says Park. That includes increasing demand for precision blasting technologies and challenges as mines go deeper and miners seek to maintain output volumes. "However, we think it is prudent to remain conservative," particularly on Dyno Nobel's EMEA and Latam unit, or DNEL, given the company doesn't have manufacturing capacity in those regions, Park says. Dyno Nobel rose 4.8% Wednesday to A$3.25. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1902 ET [Dow Jones]--Australian stocks are set to rise at the open, with ASX SPI200 futures up by 0.5% before the bell. The market will have tailwinds from Wall Street, where stocks extended recent gains. "Financial markets were largely unperturbed as the U.S. government entered a shutdown," Westpac senior economist Mantas Vanagas says in a note. Rather, sentiment has been buoyed by expectations of more Fed rate cuts this year. Australia's gold stocks are likely to get a boost from a continued rally in gold prices, which trade at a record high. Other minerals including copper, silver and iron ore are also higher, providing tailwinds for the resource-rich market, although oil prices are down. Ahead of trading, ARN Media named Chief Operating Officer Michael Stephenson as its next CEO, succeeding Ciaran Davis. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1100 ET - Rivian's 3Q deliveries topped Baird's models, but production fell short of the firm's estimates, analysts say in a research note. They add the recent quarter was affected by factory downtime associated with the launch of a new vehicle model, set to debut next year. The electric-vehicle startup also narrowed and lowered its full-year guidance for deliveries. The report doesn't move the needle for the company, whose catalysts for growth lie in the upcoming R2 launch and an in-house developed autonomy strategy, the analysts say. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1057 ET - Rivian posts higher-than-expected deliveries in 3Q and narrows its full-year delivery outlook. The new outlook is in-line with Benchmark's model, analysts from the firm say in a research note. It will also provide a cushion going into 4Q, when deliveries are expected to normalize after pull-forward in demand during 3Q tied to policy and incentive timing in select markets, the analysts say. "Our focus remains beyond near-term swings and on the 2026 mid-size platform launch and software monetization as key drivers of the profitability path," they write. Shares fall 8.1%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1036 ET - Tesla's strong rebound in deliveries during 3Q partly reflects a pull-forward in demand tied to the expiration of electric-vehicle tax incentives, Benchmark analysts say in a research note. Brand momentum and a refreshed lineup, though, likely did heavier lifting, they add. "Given the stock's resilience into the release, we see no change to our thesis and believe astute investors are looking beyond near-term delivery volatility to higher-margin software/services in automation and robotics," the analysts write. Tesla's energy business is scaling as well, supporting the investment case, they note. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1032 ET - Tesla's better-than-expected 3Q deliveries were fueled by a solid rebound in China and a pull-forward in demand in the U.S. before electric-vehicle tax incentives shut off, Wedbush analysts say in a note. "While EV demand is expected to fall with the EV tax credit expiration, this was a great bounceback quarter for Tesla to lay the groundwork for deliveries moving forward, but there is still work to do to gain further ground from a delivery perspective," they write. Despite the strong delivery numbers, the analysts say that investors remain more focused on Tesla's next chapter as an artificial-intelligence and autonomous company, given the market opportunity these initiatives represent. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1024 ET - Tesla delivered more than 497,000 vehicles in 3Q, materially beating estimates, Baird analysts Ben Kallo and Davis Sunderland say in a research note. "Street estimates moved higher into the end of quarter, but strong sales of the Model Y and 3 beat even heightened expectations," they write, noting that energy deployments also topped forecasts. The analysts note that, despite the beats, the electric-vehicle maker could see some near-term noise around tariffs when it reports quarterly results later this month. "But we continue to think investor focus is on the longer term," the analysts say. "We would be buyers of the stock ahead of numerous catalysts ahead." Tesla ticks down 1%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0832 ET - - Ferrari will probably deliver a promise of growth at its investor day next week, HSBC analysts write. The bank expects the management to lay out its plans to 2030. HSBC sees scope for volumes to grow further, personalization to increase and profitability to enjoy a significant bump from the F80 and 296 Speciale models. On top of mid-single-digit revenue growth, HSBC thinks recent efforts on digitalization and the ramp-up of production should help efficiency, pushing EBIT margins up to 32% in 2027. Overall, the bank sees an earnings compound annual growth rate of 11% out to 2030. HSBC upgrades the stock to buy from hold and raises its target price to 470 euros from 413 euros. Shares rise 2.6% to 424.90 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0552 ET - European defense stocks rise in Thursday morning trade as European leaders gather in Copenhagen to discuss security cooperation, following suspected Russian drone incursions across NATO airspace. Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen calls for action to deter Russia, not just words. "It must be clear to everyone now, Russia will not stop until they are forced to do so," she says, opening the proceedings. Thales is up 4.1%, Indra Sistemas gains 3.2%, Renk Group advances 3.1%, Hensoldt grows 2.3%, Safran rises 1.8%, Dassault Aviation gains 1.75%, Leonardo increases 1.5%, BAE Systems moves up 1.1%, and Rheinmetall edges up 1%. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0345 ET - Stellantis reported a 6.4% jump in third-quarter U.S. sales to 324,825 vehicles, sending shares higher. "Fueled by sales growth across the Jeep, Ram, Chrysler and Fiat brands, our U.S. sales saw strong results in the third quarter, including the month of September, which was our highest monthly market share in the U.S. in 15 months," said Jeff Kommor, head of U.S. sales. Stellantis said it is taking action to keep sales momentum moving forward, including bringing back a V8-powered Ram truck, and introducing a new Dodge Charger Scat Pack and Jeep Cherokee. Jeep brand sales rose 11% on year to 160,564 vehicles, while Ram sales fell 4.8% to 103,717 vehicles. Chrysler sales rose 45%, Dodge sales fell 1.7%, Fiat sales rose 1.6% and Alfa Romeo sales fell 21%. Shares rise 6%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0256 ET - Air France-KLM's third-quarter EBIT could come at 1.17 billion euros, below Bloomberg consensus of 1.36 billion euros, J.P. Morgan analysts say. This result would be broadly similar to last year's, they say. Underlying group pricing will be the key focus of the airline group's results, alongside Transavia's pricing, they note. "We view consensus expectations as having better caught up with current events, and so the risk-reward on the shares in our view looks less positive with regards to earnings momentum, against a share price which is [up] 40% year-to-date", the analysts write in a note.(cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0253 ET - Nio's strong September sales suggest that it will post a narrower quarterly loss, Deutsche Bank analyst Bin Wang says in a note. The Shanghai-based EV maker reported record-high monthly deliveries of 34,749 in September, up 64% on year and 11% on month. That pushes 3Q volumes to a 87,071 units--also a new high--up 41% on year and 21% on quarter, Wang says. The analyst expects Nio's quarterly net loss to narrow 22% sequentially to about CNY4.0 billion as a result of the robust sales. DB maintains a buy rating and a target price of HK$82.00 on Nio's H-shares, which were recently at HK$61.50. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0251 ET - International Consolidated Airlines Group--which houses British Airways and Iberia, among others--faces the lowest risk among European carriers of long-haul overcapacity on its routes into the winter season, J.P. Morgan analysts say. Out of the three European flag carriers--IAG, Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa--IAG has the most favorable balance between demand and supply, the analysts say. Transatlantic capacity remains constrained, particularly in the U.K.-U.S. market which is IAG's largest profit pool, they say. IAG remains JPM's top pick among European airlines because the group has potential to continue rewarding shareholders thanks to its strong free-cash-flow generation, the analysts say. JPM retains its overweight rating on IAG, neutral for Air France-KLM, and underweight for Lufthansa. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0123 ET - Hyundai Motor India appears to lack an aggressive strategy in the country's automotive market, HDFC Securities analysts say in a note, as the brokerage initiates coverage of the stock with a reduce rating and a target price of INR2,236.00. The automaker's market share fell to 13.9% in FY 2025 from 17.4% in FY 2016 as competitors strengthened their presence in the sport-utility vehicle segment and rolled out more advanced technology and features, the brokerage notes. HDFC Securities says it sees some "green shoots" of a more aggressive strategy, with management planning 26 model launches by FY 2030, but remains cautious for now. The brokerage will closely monitor any changes in strategy that could help revive the company's standing in India. Its shares last closed at INR2,537.05. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: CME cattle fall on profit-taking
data.description:
By Heather Schlitz

Chicago Mercantile Exchange cattle futures fell on profit-taking on Thursday, though hopes for the border reopening to Mexican cattle imports have also put a damper on prices.

On Tuesday, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration announced it had approved Dectomax-CA1, an injectable anti-parasitic medication, to prevent and treat screwworm in cattle.

However, Mexico saw a nearly 32% increase in confirmed screwworm cases, according to the latest monthly government data, as a concentration of cases moves north.

"It's starting to weigh on feeder cattle," Karl Setzer, partner at Consus Ag, said.

CME December live cattle (LCZ25) fell 1.025 cents to end at 233.475 cents per pound. CME November feeder cattle (FCX25) fell 5.2 cents to end at 352.40 cents per pound.

CME December hogs (LHZ25) closed 0.225 cent lower to end at 86.675 cents per pound. A lack of buying interest has weighed on lean hog futures.

Wholesale beef prices have mostly continued a seasonal decline. The U.S. Department of Agriculture reported choice cuts of beef fell by $5.37 to end at $363.10 per hundredweight as of Thursday morning. Select cuts fell by $2.36 to $344.53.

Commodity markets may be left without further USDA data in the coming days after a U.S. government shutdown started on Wednesday, amid political deadlock over short-term funding measures.

data.title: Soybeans rebound as market weighs China prospects
data.description:
Key points:
  • Soybeans higher on hopes for Chinese demand
  • Wheat rises on good demand, corn follows wheat higher

By Heather Schlitz

Chicago soybean futures ticked higher on Thursday, continuing the previous day's rebound as the market assessed chances for a resumption in Chinese demand following comments by U.S. President Donald Trump, analysts said.

Corn and wheat, which like soybeans hit multiweek lows on Wednesday, extended a bounce, with help from a weaker dollar and signs of fresh demand from wheat importers.

The most-active soybean contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) settled 10-3/4 cents higher at $10.23-3/4 per bushel. On Wednesday, the contract fell below $10 for the first time in seven weeks before rebounding by the close.

U.S. farmers have lost out on billions of dollars of sales to China, which is the biggest soy importer but has not yet bought beans from the autumn U.S. harvest amid a trade war with Washington.

Trump said in a social media post on Wednesday that soybeans would be a major topic of discussion when he meets Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in four weeks.

"That gave soybeans support yesterday and we're building on that today," Karl Setzer, partner at Consus Ag, said. Rising soymeal prices have also provided support for soybeans.

The post spurred some buying, but ample supply and China's continued absence from the U.S. market will likely cap any gains.

Ignoring the U.S., China has ramped up soybean purchases from South America, including a large volume of Argentine soybeans last week during a brief export tax waiver.

CBOT corn settled 5-1/4 cents higher at $4.21-1/2 per bushel, and CBOT wheat settled 5-1/2 cents higher at $5.14-3/4 per bushel.

Strong demand for wheat and signs of planting delays for winter wheat in the U.S. Plains have given a boost to wheat futures, with corn following wheat higher.

A U.S. government shutdown since Wednesday, amid political deadlock over budget funding, will likely delay the release of key USDA data.

Police use tear gas and water canons on Gaza protest in Geneva
data.description:
By Olivia Le Poidevin and Emma Farge

Police sprayed tear gas on protesters in Geneva, Switzerland on Thursday evening who were marching in support of the Gaza flotilla, eyewitnesses told Reuters, which the police confirmed.

"We were retreating and suddenly got heavily gassed," a eye witness told Reuters, describing a burning sensation in their eyes and difficult breathing.

Israel's interception of the flotilla sparked protests in Geneva and Bern, as well as in Italy and Colombia on Thursday.

Israeli forces intercepted around 40 boats carrying aid and more than 400 foreign activists, including some Swiss nationals, and Swedish campaigner Greta Thunberg, to Gaza

A photo shared with Reuters by a eyewitness in Geneva showed a large plume of white smoke coming from a street filled with protesters, which they described as tear gas.

A second eyewitness told Reuters that they saw a large white vehicle spray water on the front line of protesters, which a video shared by the Swiss national broadcaster RTS also showed.

Alexandre Brahier, spokesperson for the Geneva police said that around 3,000 people, mostly adults and young men had joined the protest.

"We had to use control measures including tear gas and water canons," he said, adding that protesters were not carrying dangerous arms but were "throwing things", causing some damage to property spraying graffiti.

Such confrontations are rare for Switzerland although pro-Gaza protests have been gathering momentum in recent weeks.

"Large protests are to be expected in big cities but it’s not every day that we need to resort to control measures like this," Brahier added.

Mobile phone footage shared with Reuters show flares being thrown amidst the crowd, as hundreds of people moving down a crammed street in central Geneva.

data.title: Coursera CFO to Leave Role
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Coursera Chief Financial Officer Ken Hahn will leave his role, effective Oct. 29, and become an adviser to the company.

The online learning company said it had begun a search to identify a permanent replacement.

Hahn has served as CFO of Coursera since 2020 and is credited with leading the company's successful initial public offering in 2021. He will stay on as an adviser for one year.

Last year, Hahn's total compensation was $9.2 million, including a base salary of $446,833, according to an annual filing from the company.

The company reaffirmed its previous guidance for the third quarter and full year.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Taking Credit — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

What Happened in Markets Today:

OpenAI reaches $500 billion valuation. A group of investors paid about $6.6 billion to buy shares owned by OpenAI employees, valuing the company at $500 billion. The company also reached deals with Korean companies Samsung and SK Hynix as memory-chip partners to the Stargate infrastructure project.

Chip stocks rose. The rally wasn't limited to Samsung and SK Hynix, which rose 3.5% and 9.9%, respectively. AMD and Intel shares gained 3.5% and 3.8%, respectively.

Tesla hit a sales record. The company's global EV deliveries rose 7.4% in the third quarter compared with a year earlier as U.S. consumers rushed to take advantage of the $7,500 federal EV credit. The credit expired at the end of the quarter. Tesla shares fell 5.1%.

FICO introduced a program that cuts out credit bureaus. The program would let mortgage lenders buy credit scores directly from the company, bypassing Experian, Equifax and TransUnion. FICO shares surged 18%, making it the top performer in the S&P 500. TransUnion and Equifax shares fell 11% and 8.5%, respectively. Experian was down 4.2%.

The government shutdown continued a second day. Lawmakers remain deadlocked over spending. Prediction markets suggest that bettors think the government shutdown will last until at least the end of next week.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Turkey may work jointly with US, S.Korea for its second nuclear power plant, minister says
data.description:
Turkey may work with the United States and South Korea for its second planned nuclear power plant, Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar said on Thursday, adding this may be in the form of a trilateral model.

Ankara has said it was in talks with Russia, China, Canada and South Korea on possible new nuclear power plants, in the northern Sinop and Western Thrace regions, to add to the Akkuyu nuclear power plant it is building with Russia.

Speaking to broadcaster CNN Turk, Bayraktar said President Tayyip Erdogan had discussed cooperation on both small and big reactors with the leaders of Canada and France, adding that the country would cooperate with the United States for both small modular and conventional plant development after a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House last month.

"We can say the United States and (South) Korea got added together. Therefore, there may be a trilateral model with Korea-America-Turkey," he said, while repeating that the first reactor of Akkuyu would be operational in 2026.

Bayraktar also said Turkey wanted access to cheap energy and technology transfer, know-how with such investments, and that an accord providing these was reached with Russia on Akkuyu, so it would also seek the same for its second nuclear power plant.
